section.title: What is xdock?
section.para: xdock is a software that displays small applets (also called docks) on the side of the screen. It seeks to emulate the WindowMaker dock applets.
The motivation for this project is to present a more modern platform to develop the dock applets. The WindowMaker docks, while great, have some shortcommings:

itemizedlist.listitem: it's hard to write applets, since it requires knowledge in Xlib, that most users don't have.
WindowMaker dock applets are too tightly integrated into WindowMaker itself, and don't work very well under other window managers, and much less under other operating systems.

section.para: xdock seeks to address these shortcommings by offering a dock server, where the docks themselves are clients that connect to this dock. Since the docks (the clients) and the server communicate using a standard protocol, xdock can easily be ported to other platfoms. The communication protocol is very simple, and applets can be built in any language since they respect the protocol. xdock also offers a C library, so that docks can be easily written in C.

section.title: Invoking xdock
section.para: xdock server can be easily started with the following command:

listitem.para: Choose the color theme that will be used by xdockserver. The color theme define the default colors that'll be drawn on the applet. The default theme is led, where the applet will resemble a green LED panel. The alternative theme lcd will make the dock look like a LCD panel.
Choose the color of the background of the applet. This option can be used to match the color of the applet with the theme being used by the window manager. The color must be in the format #RRGGBB.
When the dock is dragged to the right border of the screen, it'll be automaticly attracted by n_pixels.

The IP addresses the server will listen to. The default value is localhost, which means the server will only accept clients that connect from the same machine the server is running. The alternative parameter is all, where the server will accept connections from any clients.
The IP port the server will listen to. The default value is 52530. If a different port is set, the same argument must be set on the clients.

When this option is set, the server will write debugging information about everything, including all raw communication with the clients. The information will be output to stdout. This is useful when writting a new client, to debug communication.

section.title: Config file
section.para: A config file can be created to store the options explained above. The config file will have the name ~/.xdock/config.rc and the options will be stored in the file in the format option = value. For options that don't have a value (such as debug), use true as the value.

section.title: Client options
section.para: Each client will have its own arguments, that usually can be discovered with -h or --help. But they are required to present at least the following options:

listitem.para: The address where this client will connect to the xdock server. The default value is localhost.
The IP port where the client will connect to the server. The default value is 52530.
